query: As of July 2026, has any U.S. Supreme Court justice announced retirement or resignation? Status of Alito, Thomas retirement speculation after NPR retracted its June 30 2026 story. Any current plans for a justice to retire in summer 2026.
answer:
I've completed the research. Here's the summary:

**As of July 12, 2026, no U.S. Supreme Court justice has announced retirement or resignation.**

- **The NPR retraction (June 30, 2026):** NPR erroneously reported Justice Alito was retiring; the story was live only ~5-6 minutes before being retracted. The error came from correspondent Nina Totenberg mishearing a bystander's "retirement announcements" as a singular reference to Alito. The Supreme Court's Public Information Office explicitly denied it.

- **Alito (76):** Not retiring. Sources (ABC, CBS, April 2026) say he intends to serve into at least 2027 and has been hiring clerks for next term.

- **Thomas (77):** Does not plan to step down, per sources close to him.

- **No 2026 vacancy expected**, meaning Trump cannot currently plan a fourth SCOTUS nomination before the November 2026 midterms.

- **Market signals:** Polymarket odds of Alito announcing retirement by end of 2026 were ~14.5% on July 12, down sharply from above-50% levels seen on Kalshi during April's peak speculation.

The findings have been submitted via the StructuredOutput tool with full key facts, sources, and quantitative data points.

sources.title: NPR retracts story about Alito retirement
sources.snippet: NPR legal affairs correspondent Nina Totenberg misheard an announcement; story published 10:51 a.m. and removed by 10:57 a.m. ET on June 30, 2026.

Justice Alito retirement story retracted by NPR after false report
Samuel Alito Is Not Retiring From Supreme Court—NPR Retracts Story Published In Error
sources.snippet: Alito, 76, briefly hospitalized in April; speculation continued but he has not retired.

sources.title: Supreme Court denies report that Justice Samuel Alito is retiring
sources.snippet: The Supreme Court's public information office is denying a published, since-retracted report that the court announced Alito's retirement.

sources.snippet: Alito intends to continue serving into at least 2027 and has been hiring clerks for next term, sources said.

sources.title: Supreme Court Justices Alito and Thomas not planning to retire this year, sources say
sources.snippet: Sources close to Alito (76) and Thomas (77) say neither plans to step down in 2026; article dated April 17, 2026.

sources.title: Will Samuel Alito announce his retirement by...? (Polymarket)
sources.snippet: Probability ~14.5% on July 12, 2026; ranged 12%-22.5% over July 5-12; ~$567K volume.

sources.title: Samuel Alito's Odds of Retiring to Allow Trump New Supreme Court Pick
sources.snippet: During April speculation, Kalshi placed odds of Alito retiring before Jan 1 at 50.6 percent.

key_facts: No sitting U.S. Supreme Court justice has announced retirement or resignation as of July 12, 2026.
NPR published an erroneous story on June 30, 2026 reporting Justice Alito was retiring; it was live only ~5-6 minutes (published ~10:51 a.m. ET, removed by ~10:57 a.m. ET) before retraction.
The NPR error was caused by correspondent Nina Totenberg mishearing 'retirement announcements' (plural, routine) as a singular 'announcement' implying Alito.
The Supreme Court Public Information Office explicitly denied that any Alito retirement had been announced.
Justice Samuel Alito (age 76, on Court since 2006) is not retiring; sources say he intends to serve into at least 2027 and has hired clerks for the next term (ABC, CBS, April 2026).
Justice Clarence Thomas (age 77, on Court since 1991) does not plan to step down, per sources close to him (CBS, April 2026).
No Supreme Court vacancy is expected in 2026, so Trump cannot currently plan a fourth nomination before the November 2026 midterms.
Alito was briefly hospitalized in Philadelphia in April 2026, which fueled but did not confirm retirement speculation.
Polymarket odds of Alito announcing retirement by end of 2026 were about 14.5% on July 12, 2026, down from above 50% (Kalshi) during peak April speculation.

data_points.metric: Duration NPR erroneous Alito retirement story was live
Polymarket probability Alito announces retirement by end of 2026
Kalshi probability Alito retires before Jan 1, 2027 (peak April speculation)
Number of sitting Supreme Court justices who have announced retirement/resignation
